target.name: task-plan
target.source_summary: templates/task-plan.md + agents/planner.md + skills/planning/decompose-tasks.md + planner Technology Decision Framework

gates.description: The task dependency graph is acyclic. A plan with a cycle is not executable in any order — no believable middle exists.

The plan does not re-decide or contradict encoded platform policy: no splitting features between Pages Functions and Workers (consistency principle), no model-routing choices contradicting the configured Mastra model policy without stated cause, no crypto choices contradicting the PBKDF2 standard. Policy before prompt is constitution rule 1.
Every task row has a non-empty owner role and deliverable.

dimensions.description: Tasks are implementable deliverables, not themes.

anchors.1: Tasks are themes: 'set up auth', 'work on billing' — no named deliverable; decompose-tasks anti-pattern verbatim.
anchors.3: Deliverables named, but at least one task is too large to review in a single pass, or mixes layers (frontend+backend+storage) with no split rationale.
anchors.5: Every task names one reviewable deliverable ('implement login endpoint returning JWT'), sized to be implemented, reviewed, and accepted independently.

dimensions.description: Criteria can be verified by inspection, test, or demonstration — not subjective judgment.

anchors.1: Criteria restate the task title ('done when login works') or are absent.
anchors.3: Most criteria checkable, but at least one is subjective ('works well', 'is clean') with no test stated.
anchors.5: Every criterion is pass/fail checkable without re-interpreting intent; error cases included where the deliverable has failure modes.

dimensions.description: Every consumes-output relation between tasks is declared by ID.

anchors.1: Dependencies absent or 'none' everywhere despite obvious ordering (handlers before the schema they read).
anchors.3: Declared by ID, but at least one implied dependency is missing — a task consumes another's output without depending on it.
anchors.5: Every consumes-output relation declared by ID; reading the deliverables surfaces no hidden dependency.

dimensions.description: Tasks name the files or surfaces they will change, or explicitly flag unknown.

anchors.1: No task names files or surfaces.
anchors.3: Most tasks name surfaces; the rest are silent rather than explicitly marked unknown.
anchors.5: Every task names its surfaces or states 'unknown' with why — boundary drift is preventable from the plan alone.

dimensions.description: Architecture and technology choices carry rationale traceable to the decision frameworks.

anchors.1: Technology choices asserted with no rationale, or absent where the work clearly requires them.
anchors.3: Choices present with generic rationale ('Workers is better for this').
anchors.5: Deployment model, components, data layer, and model capability per feature each justified via the frameworks: dependency-chain test for Workflows, consistency principle, fewer-deployments preference, and configured Mastra model-routing policy.

dimensions.description: Decision log contains only genuine blockers, each decision-shaped.

anchors.1: Decision log padded with non-blockers, or empty while the plan visibly guesses at unclear intent.
anchors.3: Real blockers logged, but mixed with items resolvable by a safe assumption.
anchors.5: Only genuine blockers; each has topic, why it matters, options considered, and follow-up impact (decision-log template shape).

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: task_concreteness is heaviest because 'broad themes instead of implementable tasks' is the planner's named Must Not and the failure that makes every downstream stage unworkable — a vague plan poisons build, test, and review at once.

known_good.content: Scope: email/password login. T1 (engineer): sessions+users schema migration, *.sql; depends none; accept: migration applies cleanly, columns CHECK-constrained, FKs indexed. T2 (engineer): POST /api/login returning session cookie; src/utils/auth.js + functions/api/login.js; depends T1; accept: 200+cookie on valid creds, 401 with actionable body on invalid, PBKDF2-100k verified by inspection, release gate covers happy path/401/429 without arbitrary waits. Tech: Pages-vs-Workers not re-decided (policy: existing model retained). Open decisions: session length 30d default assumed — confirm only if product disagrees. Risks: rate-limit storage choice deferred to T2.

known_bad.summary: Theme plan that also re-decides settled policy.
known_bad.content: Scope: auth. T1: Set up authentication (owner: engineer). Done when auth works. T2: Make the login UI nice. Depends: T1. T3: We should move the login form to Pages Functions while keeping the API in Workers for speed. Open decisions: none.
